claim: The anomalously high hapax ratio (70.1%) is substantially produced by a systematic word-final suffix drawn from the set {y, n, l, r, o} that is a cipher artifact (e.g., a null, padding, or word-delimiter glyph), such that stripping that final glyph from all words reduces the unique-word count by at least 30%.
evidence_for: Word-final glyphs {y,n,l,r,o} account for the dominant positional constraint, suggesting a structural rather than grammatical role
High hapax ratio is inconsistent with natural-language Zipf scaling at exponent 0.8946, pointing to an artificial source of surface type diversity
Existing belief at 0.54 already partially supports suffix-stripping reducing hapax count
If final glyphs are padding or nulls, stripping them would collapse many hapax forms to shared stems
evidence_against: Stripping final glyphs from Latin or Italian words would also be linguistically valid (case/conjugation endings), so reduction might reflect grammar not artifact
Word-final {y,n,l,r,o} could encode real Latin endings (-us, -um, -is, -am, -re)
Recipes section has the largest word count and second-highest entropy, suggesting some sections have genuine suffix variation
test: For each unique word form ending in any glyph from {y,n,l,r,o}, create a stemmed vocabulary by removing the final glyph; compute new vocabulary size, new hapax count, and new hapax ratio. Run the same procedure on the Currier A and B sub-corpora separately.
test_metric: Reduction in hapax ratio after final-glyph stripping: (original_hapax_count - stemmed_hapax_count) / original_hapax_count
test_threshold: Confirm artifact hypothesis if stripping reduces hapax ratio by >= 30 percentage points (from 70.1% to <= 40%); deny if reduction < 10 percentage points, suggesting final glyphs encode real morphological information

tests_run.test: for each unique word form ending in any glyph from {y,n,l,r,o}, create a stemmed vocabulary by remov
tests_run.details: Language A: entropy=3.8321, 3513 unique/11022 total words. Language B: entropy=3.8611, 5089 unique/23766 total words. Entropy difference: 0.0290. Vocabulary density difference: 0.1046.

reasoning: If the hapax explosion is artificial and caused by a trailing null or padding glyph, a single suffix-stripping pass should dramatically collapse the vocabulary. This test is fully computable from the word list and would immediately distinguish cipher artifact from natural morphological diversity.
